tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Laboratory results are largely within normal limits. Mildly decreased total CO₂ may suggest a tendency toward mild metabolic acidosis, but clinical correlation is needed. Cholesterol and LDL are slightly elevated, indicating increased cardiovascular risk; lipid management may be considered. Renal function, glucose control, and urine microalbumin/creatinine ratio are normal. No acute laboratory concerns identified.

display.value: Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
